course.title: Lesson 41: Do You Call That a Hat

segments.text: Do you call that a hat?
First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: What kind of shopping does the writer enjoy, do you think?

translations.zh-Hans: 你认为作者喜欢哪一种购物？

segments.text: Do you call that a hat?
You needn't be so rude about it!
My wife answered as she looked at herself in the mirror.

translations.zh-Hans: 妻子一边照镜子一边回答。

segments.text: I sat down on one of those modern chairs with holes in it and waited.

translations.zh-Hans: 我坐在一把带孔的现代椅子上等着。

segments.text: We had been in the hat shop for half an hour, and my wife was still in front of the mirror.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们在帽子店里已经待了半个小时，妻子仍站在镜子前。

segments.text: We mustn't buy things we don't need, I remarked suddenly.

translations.zh-Hans: “我们不该买不需要的东西，”我突然说道。

segments.text: I regretted saying it almost at once.

translations.zh-Hans: 话一出口我几乎立刻就后悔了。

segments.text: You needn't have said that, my wife answered.

translations.zh-Hans: “你本来不必说这句话，”妻子回答，

segments.text: I needn't remind you of that terrible tie you bought yesterday.

translations.zh-Hans: “我不必提醒你昨天买的那条糟糕的领带吧。”

segments.text: I find it beautiful, I said.
A man can never have too many ties.
And a woman can't have too many hats, she answered.

translations.zh-Hans: “女人有再多帽子也不嫌多，”她回答。

segments.text: Ten minutes later, we walked out of the shop together.

translations.zh-Hans: 十分钟后，我们一起走出了商店。

segments.text: My wife was wearing a hat that looked like a lighthouse.

translations.zh-Hans: 妻子戴着一顶看起来像灯塔的帽子。
